name: Forecast Coach — Workato Native Blueprint
summary: Provides AI-powered coaching insights for sales leaders by analyzing their team's open pipeline each week. Native-first Workato blueprint that favors recipe functions, custom connectors, and native delivery/connectivity surfaces over raw HTTP steps.

native_connectors_required: Backstory custom connector built with the Connector SDK
SMTP-compatible email connector for outbound delivery
Lookup tables, data tables, or project properties for routing and canonical maps
prohibited_patterns: No repeated Universal HTTP or ad hoc API request actions for Slack, CRM, calendar, or email delivery
No credentials stored in formula literals, project properties comments, or recipe notes
No agent output sent straight to transport or records without JSON schema validation
No routing, dedupe, or retry logic hidden inside opaque code snippets when Recipe Functions or data tables can own it

connectors: SMTP-compatible email connector for outbound delivery

recipe_functions.purpose: Normalize the Forecast Coach trigger into the shared run_context contract.
Load and normalize the primary Forecast Coach business inputs into source_record objects.
Join the intermediate context needed for Forecast Coach, especially Pull Team Pipeline, Compile Leader Reports.
Render normalized delivery_payload objects for Forecast Coach.
Capture deterministic summary, dedupe, and retry state for Forecast Coach.

steps: Use Backstory custom connector action for Pull Team Pipeline
Use an LLM connector or AI step to return strict JSON for AI Deal Health Analysis
Use Backstory custom connector action for Compile Leader Reports
Use Gmail, Outlook, or SMTP connector for Deliver via Email
Record deterministic run summary and retry state

ai_deal_health_analysis.intent: AI Agent evaluates each deal across multiple dimensions (engagement, momentum, stakeholder mapping, competitive signals) and generates coaching-ready insights.

deployment_notes: Store connector configuration, defaults, and destination IDs in connection objects or project properties.
Expose Backstory through a reusable custom connector instead of repeated HTTP actions.
Use lookup tables or data tables for routing maps, dedupe keys, and canonical mappings.
Keep synthesis agentic, but keep delivery and persistence deterministic.
